name: Silverstone Circuit
summary: The home of British motorsport and the British Grand Prix, built on a former WWII airfield.

name: Brands Hatch
summary: A natural amphitheatre circuit in Kent, former host of the British Grand Prix.

name: Donington Park
summary: One of Britain's oldest permanent circuits, host to British Superbikes and historic racing.

name: Oulton Park
summary: A fast, undulating parkland circuit in Cheshire set around a country estate.

name: Snetterton Circuit
summary: Norfolk's fast airfield circuit, known for its long straights and heavy braking zones.

name: Thruxton Circuit
summary: The fastest circuit in the UK, averaging well over 110 mph a lap in touring cars.

name: Cadwell Park
summary: The 'Mini-Nürburgring' — a narrow, hilly circuit famous for bikes jumping at The Mountain.

name: Knockhill Racing Circuit
summary: Scotland's national motorsport centre, a short and dramatic circuit in the Fife hills.

name: Croft Circuit
summary: North Yorkshire's airfield circuit with a long rallycross heritage.

name: Anglesey Circuit (Trac Môn)
summary: A spectacular coastal circuit overlooking the Irish Sea and Snowdonia.

name: Castle Combe Circuit
summary: One of the longest continually operating circuits in the UK, largely unchanged since 1950.

name: Mallory Park
summary: 'The friendly circuit' — a short lakeside track with the famous Gerard's long right-hander.

name: Santa Pod Raceway
summary: Europe's first permanent drag strip and home of European championship drag racing.

name: Shelsley Walsh Hill Climb
summary: The world's oldest motorsport venue still using its original course, first run in 1905.

name: Prescott Speed Hill Climb
summary: The Bugatti Owners' Club hill climb in the Cotswolds, a fixture since 1938.

name: Goodwood Motor Circuit
summary: Historic circuit preserved in its 1960s form, home of the Revival and Festival of Speed hill climb.

name: Lydden Hill Race Circuit
summary: The birthplace of rallycross — the sport was invented here in 1967.

name: Pembrey Circuit
summary: 'The home of Welsh motorsport' — a busy airfield circuit hosting cars, bikes and rallycross.

name: Harewood Speed Hillclimb
summary: Yorkshire's speed hillclimb venue, run by the BARC on the Harewood estate.

name: Gurston Down Speed Hillclimb
summary: A fast, sweeping downhill-start hillclimb on a Wiltshire farm estate.

name: Doune Hillclimb
summary: Scotland's premier hillclimb course, set in the grounds of Cambusmore estate.

name: Loton Park Hill Climb
summary: A parkland hillclimb through the deer park of Loton Hall in Shropshire.

name: Wiscombe Park Hill Climb
summary: A wooded Devon hillclimb famous for its Martini and Sawbench corners.

name: Craigantlet Hill Climb
summary: Northern Ireland's round of the British championship, run on closed public roads.

name: Bouley Bay Hill Climb
summary: A dramatic climb from a Jersey harbour up cliffside hairpins.

name: Val des Terres Hill Climb
summary: Guernsey's historic hillclimb rising out of St Peter Port.

name: Aintree Motor Racing Circuit
summary: Aintree Motor Racing Circuit is a 3.000 mi (4.828 km) motor-racing circuit in the village of Aintree, Metropolitan Borough of Sefton, Merseyside, England. The circuit is within the Aintree Racecourse, and used the same grandstands as horse racing.

name: Crimond Raceway
summary: Crimond Raceway is the most northerly stock car track in the United Kingdom. It is located about a mile from the village of Crimond in Aberdeenshire, Scotland.

name: Cwmythig Hill
summary: Circuit in Powys.

name: Driftland
summary: Driftland is a purpose-built race track for drifting at the Lochgelly Motorsport Complex, Fife, Scotland, inspired by the drift tracks of Japan.

name: Eddie Wright Raceway
summary: The Eddie Wright Raceway is a British race track in Scunthorpe, North Lincolnshire. It is primarily used for motorcycle speedway, but also occasionally hosts stock car racing.

name: ExCeL Street Circuit
summary: Circuit in England, first used in 2021.

name: Foxhill motocross circuit
summary: Foxhill motocross circuit is a well-known motocross circuit situated near to the small village of Foxhill, around six miles south east of Swindon, Wiltshire, England.

name: Hednesford Hills Raceway
summary: Hednesford Hills Raceway is an oval, short-circuit motor racing venue. It is situated on Cannock Chase, approximately 1-mile (1.6 km) from the town of Hednesford, Staffordshire, England and is operated by Incarace Ltd.

name: King's Lynn Stadium
summary: King's Lynn Stadium, also known as the Adrian Flux Arena and previously as the Norfolk Arena, is a short oval stadium situated to the south of King's Lynn, Norfolk, England.

name: Ingliston Racing Circuit
summary: Ingliston Racing Circuit is a 1.030 mi (1.658 km) motor racing circuit that was built at the Royal Highland Showground at Ingliston, Edinburgh.

name: Kirkistown Circuit

location.town: Ards and North Down District Council

summary: Kirkistown Motor Racing Circuit is a permanent motorsport circuit located between the villages of Kirkistown and Portavogie, on the Ards Peninsula of County Down in Northern Ireland.

name: Lochgelly Raceway
summary: Circuit in Fife.

name: Mendips Raceway
summary: Mendips Raceway is a motorsport venue in the Mendip Hills in Somerset, England. It is located on the rim of Batts Combe quarry between Shipham and Charterhouse.

name: Melbourne Raceway
summary: Drag Strip in East Riding of Yorkshire.

name: Oliver's Mount racing circuit
summary: Circuit in North Yorkshire.

name: Odsal Stadium
summary: Odsal Stadium is a multipurpose stadium in Bradford, West Yorkshire, England. The stadium is currently the home of Bradford Bulls rugby league football club.

name: Snaefell Mountain Course
summary: The Isle of Man TT Mountain Course, also known as the TT Course, Snaefell Mountain Course or Elmo's Mountain Course, is a street and public rural road circuit located in the Isle of Man, used for motorcycle racing.

name: Swaffham Raceway Stadium
summary: Swaffham Raceway, originally Swaffham Stadium, is a stock car and banger racing circuit in Swaffham, Norfolk, which also hosted greyhound racing from 1987 until 2000.

name: Three Sisters Race Circuit
summary: The Three Sisters Circuit is a motor racing circuit in England, near Wigan in Greater Manchester.

name: Tandragee 100
summary: The Tandragee 100 is a motorcycle race that occurs on the public roads which connect Tandragee and Markethill, near the village of Clare, in County Armagh, Northern Ireland. Spanning 5.3 miles, it is sometimes referred to by its organisers as the 'Mini TT'.

name: Top Gear test track
summary: The Top Gear test track located at Dunsfold Aerodrome in Surrey, United Kingdom was used by the BBC automotive television programme Top Gear. The track was designed by Lotus Cars as a testing facility, with many of its Formula One cars tested there.

name: Elvington Airfield
summary: A 2-mile former RAF runway near York hosting Straightliners top-speed and land-speed record events.

name: Dakota Raceway
summary: Devon's drag strip on Smeatharpe airfield, hosting Straightliners drag racing.

name: Pendine Sands
summary: The legendary seven-mile Welsh beach where land speed records have been chased since the 1920s.

name: Dishforth Airfield
summary: Yorkshire airfield used for Straightliners top-speed and sprint events.

name: Campbeltown (Machrihanish) Airfield
summary: One of Europe's longest runways, on the Kintyre peninsula, used for top-speed events.

name: Jurby Airfield
summary: Former RAF airfield in the north of the Isle of Man hosting straight-line speed events.

name: Llanbedr Airfield
summary: A coastal Snowdonia airfield used for Straightliners speed events.

name: Solway Airfield
summary: Cumbrian airfield at Kirkbride hosting straight-line speed events.

name: Ramsey Sprint
summary: The famous promenade sprint along Ramsey's Mooragh, run during TT and Manx GP fortnights.

name: Buxton Raceway
summary: A Derbyshire stock car oval hosting BriSCA F1, F2 and Ministox racing.

name: Foxhall Stadium
summary: Suffolk's stock car stadium, home to BriSCA F1 and Hot Rod racing.

name: Mildenhall Stadium
summary: A long-running Suffolk stock car stadium hosting BriSCA F1 and 1400 Stock Cars.

name: Northampton International Raceway
summary: One of the UK's biggest stock car venues, home of the BriSCA F1 World Final.

name: Scunthorpe (Gunness) Raceway
summary: A Lincolnshire stock car stadium hosting BriSCA F1 and Hot Rod racing.

name: Skegness Stadium
summary: A Lincolnshire coastal stock car stadium, a fixture on the BriSCA F1 calendar.

name: Nutts Corner Circuit
summary: Northern Ireland's stock car venue, host of the BriSCA F1 Irish rounds.

name: Bridlington South Beach
summary: A tidal sand beach that becomes a 200-yard drag strip once a year for Race the Waves, echoing the earliest UK land-speed record runs.
